text: Failure to meet State responsibility towards detained and abandoned children is a function of lack of adequate institutional mechanism of good governance and the will of government institutions. Indonesia has a number of structural challenges which undermine the protection of children's rights as enshrined in laws and as is provided for in the International Convention on the Rights of the Child. Effective application of laws and regulations has been hindered due to weakness in the capacities of law enforcing officers as well as absence of necessary field infrastructure. In addition, social development programmes are constrained by financial difficulties as well as poor awareness of children rights and the application of the Convention on the Rights of the Child amongst parents and community members. Co-ordination between governmental institutions and non-governmental organizations is inefficient, whilst cross-sectoral and international cooperation has not yet been strengthened. The study found that a series of policy reforms and strengthening of partnerships between public and private organizations are needed to address the protection of neglected children in Indonesia.
